asset_type: TaraHome technical blog cover and article-card image
prompt: Create a unique editorial product photograph about troubleshooting an IKEA TIMMERFLOTTE temperature and humidity sensor that has gone unavailable in a local smart home. Preserve the current sensor's round white puck, softly domed face, subtle dot-matrix temperature display, shallow cylindrical edge, tiny lower ventilation holes, proportions, material, and physical geometry faithfully. Place one recognizable sensor naturally on a pale-oak shelf in a calm warm bedroom or living room with cream plaster, natural linen, one healthy leafy plant, and a restrained dark-green accent. Use clean photorealistic editorial residential photography, believable scale, soft natural morning side light, realistic shadows, and a wide landscape composition that remains clear in both 16:10 hero and centered 16:9 card crops. Keep the whole device prominent and uncropped. Do not invent controls, screen elements, ports, extra products, branding, or packaging. No embedded headline, title, watermark, diagrams, charts, graphs, decorative routing lines, translucent panels, inset frames, floating UI cards, progress bars, phone screens, dashboards, logos, dramatic sci-fi lighting, or generic AI smart-home imagery.
